summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a stellar early‑'69 set featuring monster Dark Star, Eleven and Mountains of the Moon.
review: The February 22 1969 Vallejo concert is hailed as pure psychedelic gold, with reviewers pointing to blistering versions of Dark Star, Eleven, Lovelight, Mountains of the Moon and Doin’ That Rag. The soundboard source is described as crisper than comparable tapes, delivering a clear but vintage‑era texture that benefits from a little EQ tweaking. Overall the performance is considered one of the band’s finest early‑'70s‑style outings.

ai_rating.rationale: All eight reviews are overwhelmingly positive, highlighting exceptional show moments and noting good to excellent soundboard quality; the weighted average of show (≈4.8) and recording (≈4.2) yields a strong 4.6 rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as crisper than other copies, classic early‑'69 sound; listeners recommend modest EQ to enhance clarity but report no dropouts or major flaws.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set blends late‑68 psychedelic suites with fresh Aoxomoxoa material, flowing smoothly from an expansive Dark Star into a scorching Eleven and ending on uplifting Mountains of the Moon.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His spoken intermission announcement is noted, adding to the cosmic vibe.
